title: Recent Update Causes Nextcloud-AIO Containers To Segfault (podman-init SEGV)
body:
### Issue Description

I recently performed weekly patching of my CentOS Stream 10 server. During the update I installed the latest Kernel (6.12.0), the latest Nextcloud container image, and the latest Podman (5.8.0). Since then, my Nextcloud AIO master container will start, but all of the child containers fail with SigSEGV in the podman-init process.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Install Podman on CentOS Stream 10
2. Configure systemd to have a rootful podman socket at `/run/podman/podman.sock`
3. Create a volume `nextcloud_aio_mastercontainer` (e.g. podman volume create nextcloud_aio_mastercontainer)
4. Create a data volume `nextcloud_aio_data`
5. Start `nextcloud-aio` with the following Quadlet:

```[Unit]
Requires=traefik.service
Requires=frontend-network.service
Requires=nextcloud-aio-network.service
After=network-online.target
Wants=network-online.target

[Container]
ContainerName=nextcloud-aio-mastercontainer
Image=ghcr.io/nextcloud-releases/all-in-one:latest
Volume=/run/podman/podman.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:z
Volume=nextcloud_aio_mastercontainer:/mnt/docker-aio-config
Volume=nextcloud_aio_data:/data
Environment=SKIP_DOMAIN_VALIDATION=true
SecurityLabelDisable=true
PublishPort=127.0.0.1:8080:8080

[Service]
Restart=always

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
```

6. Once that container is running, access the AIO web console on `localhost:8080` and start the containers using the button provided


### Describe the results you received

In `/var/log/messages` I see continuous attempts to start the containers which all end with entries like the following:

```
Mar  4 07:24:44 cloud systemd-coredump[500568]: Process 500490 (podman-init) of user 0 terminated abnormally with signal 11/SEGV, processing...
Mar  4 07:24:44 cloud systemd[1]: Started systemd-coredump@84472-500568-0.service - Process Core Dump (PID 500568/UID 0).
Mar  4 07:24:44 cloud podman[84560]: 2026-03-04 07:24:44.566127635 +0100 CET m=+30142.589964711 container died 7f38881c40cf43f8a6e72f9b9b3d69836ece4af7320148ef217e935ce46a51dc (image=ghcr.io/nextcloud-releases/aio-whiteboard:latest, name=nextcloud-aio-whiteboard, wud.watch=false, com.centurylinklabs.watchtower.enable=false, com.docker.compose.project=nextcloud-aio, diun.enable=false, org.label-schema.vendor=Nextcloud)
Mar  4 07:24:44 cloud podman[500268]: 2026-03-04 07:24:44.70252479 +0100 CET m=+3.063486602 container restart da24e40cb56fb505f875bca243a04e1ab25550777483fdb26f7145b08b7cb983 (image=ghcr.io/nextcloud-releases/aio-postgresql:latest, name=nextcloud-aio-database, com.docker.compose.project=nextcloud-aio, diun.enable=false, org.label-schema.vendor=Nextcloud, wud.watch=false, com.centurylinklabs.watchtower.enable=false)
Mar  4 07:24:44 cloud podman[84560]: 2026-03-04 07:24:44.63503761 +0100 CET m=+30142.658874706 image pull dc190910d33524a4e2c97f1224905e130747713be19fc06f1178e4d7e91d1992 ghcr.io/nextcloud-releases/aio-nextcloud:latest
```


### Describe the results you expected

Previously, the nextcloud-aio container would interface through the mounted podman socket and start the other containers successfully.

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

I will try to provide more details as I dig further.

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

I have also tried disabling selinux without any change.

More details:

I have **MANY** other containers running successfully on this host. Only the nextcloud controlled containers are experiencing this issue. For example, I have the following other containers running without issues:

```
root@cloud:/etc/containers# podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                        COMMAND               CREATED       STATUS       PORTS                                                                                                                               NAMES
```

What happens if you run something simple as `podman run --init docker.io/library/alpine echo test`?

`/run/podman-init` is not a podman binary but rather a bind mount of catatonit from the host which should be a static binary that can always run within the container, if that binary segfaults that might be an issue with how it was compiled for centos and unrelated to podman.

@InfoSec812 also I edited your inspect output to remove the env, you just leaked a bunch of _PASSWORD and _SECRET env values with that accidentally I assume. So I strongly recommend to rotate them if this was not a test instance.

> [@InfoSec812](https://github.com/InfoSec812) also I edited your inspect output to remove the env, you just leaked a bunch of _PASSWORD and _SECRET env values with that accidentally I assume. So I strongly recommend to rotate them if this was not a test instance.

No, this is not a live instance. These are example values from a VM environment.

As to your earlier question, other containers run fine. I suspect that nextcloud-aio is trying to use some docker API feature which is causing a problem? For example, with podman, I have to disable SECCOMP for the Collabora container because the SECCOMP data is too large for the podman API.

Reference: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/27710

@Luap99 I re-installed `catatonit` using DNF from the CentOS 10 Stream repository and restarted the containers. No improvement at all. I am still seeing the same segfault for the nextcloud-aio containers **ONLY**. All other containers are working fine.

Did you try to actually run my simple command `podman run --init docker.io/library/alpine echo test`?

It is not about other containers but container using catatonit as init which is not the default and only used with `--init` so I guess your other containers may not use that.
Yes, no errors, but also no output. Nothing in the system logs about a segfault either.

I downgraded to catatonit-5:0.2.0-1.el10 and then soft linked `/usr/libexec/catatonit/catatonit` to `/usr/libexec/podman/catatonit`. Once I did this the problem went away.
FYI looks like this is already tracked and fixed over there https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-152948 so no extra report needed from your end
@Luap99 Yes, I just found that as I was trying to submit a report! Thanks!
